# Micomicona RSS-to-Hugo Pipeline

## Architecture Overview
This project aggregates RSS feeds into a Hugo static site. The Go fetcher (`src/main.go`) parses 30 RSS feeds from `feeds.txt`, generates Markdown posts in `hugo-site/content/posts/` with Hugo frontmatter, then Hugo builds the site.

Key components:
- **Fetcher (Go)**: Parses RSS/XML, extracts images, generates posts with categories/tags
- **Hugo Site**: Uses Ananke theme, outputs HTML/RSS/JSON, serves via Nginx
- **Deployment**: `deploy.sh` compiles Go binary, runs fetcher, builds Hugo site with `--minify`

## Development Workflow
- **Build & Run**: `./deploy.sh` (compiles Go, fetches content, builds site)
- **Test Fetcher**: `cd src && go run main.go` (generates posts without full build)
- **Hugo Dev Server**: `cd hugo-site && hugo server` (live preview at localhost:1313)
- **Add Feed**: Edit `feeds.txt` (one URL per line, supports # comments)

## Code Patterns
### Post Generation (`src/main.go`)
- Filenames: Sanitized title (lowercase, remove special chars, max 60 chars) + `.md`
- Frontmatter: YAML with `title`, `date`, `categories` (source name), `tags` (auto-extracted hashtags + "Noticia"/"Agenda")
- Image Extraction: Prefers RSS media:content, falls back to HTML img tags in description
- Content: Cleaned HTML description + source link footer
- AI Summarization: Disabled (code exists for Gemini API, but commented out)

### Hugo Configuration (`hugo-site/hugo.toml`)
- Theme: Ananke (card-based layout)
- Language: Spanish (`es-es`)
- Outputs: HTML, RSS, JSON for home page
- Pagination: 24 posts per page

## Key Files
- `instrucciones.md`: Project overview and architecture docs
- `feeds.txt`: RSS feed URLs (30 feeds from rss.app)
- `hugo-site/content/posts/`: Generated Markdown files (do not edit manually)
- `hugo-site/public/`: Built static site (generated by Hugo)

## Conventions
- Posts tagged "Agenda" for events, "Noticia" for news (based on AI detection, currently disabled)
- Hashtags in content auto-converted to title-case tags
- Images: `featured_image`, `images[]`, `cover.image` all set to same URL
- Dates: Use `item.PublishedParsed` if available, else current time
- Avoid duplicates: Overwrites existing files to update content/dates

## External Dependencies
- Go modules: `gofeed` (RSS parsing), `go-sqlite3` (unused), `goquery` (HTML parsing)
- Hugo theme: Ananke (installed in `hugo-site/themes/`)
- Deployment: Assumes Ubuntu/Debian with Go, Hugo, Nginx installed
